answer: Go to Calendar, click any time slot, pick the client, service, and staff member, then confirm the date and time. New appointments default to "Booked" status.
Click the appointment on the calendar and select Edit to change the date/time. You can also drag-and-drop the appointment card directly to a new slot in Day or Week view.
Click the appointment in the calendar and select Cancel or Delete, then confirm. Cancelled bookings are logged but removed from the active calendar view.

Booked = created, unconfirmed.
Confirmed = client confirmed.
In Progress = currently being served.
Completed = done and paid.
Cancelled = appointment cancelled.
No Show = client didn't arrive.
When creating or editing an appointment, use the Notes field to add special instructions. These are visible to the assigned staff member before and during the appointment.
Go to Calendar and switch to Day View. Use the date navigation arrows or click the date to jump to a specific day. All appointments for that day appear in the time grid.
Yes — when creating an appointment, you can add multiple services. The total duration is calculated automatically by adding up each service's duration time.
Yes — when creating or editing an appointment, select a colour from the colour picker. This helps you visually identify different service types or staff on the calendar.
Click the appointment on the calendar and change its status to No Show. This logs the missed appointment against the client's profile for future reference.
In Calendar, click the Week View toggle in the top bar. You'll see all staff columns side by side across 7 days, making it easy to spot availability gaps.
You can leave the client field empty when creating an appointment. The booking will still appear on the calendar and can be linked to a client later when they arrive.
Click the appointment and change its status to Confirmed. This is typically done after the client acknowledges the appointment via call or WhatsApp.
In the Calendar top bar, use the staff filter to select a specific team member. The calendar will show only their appointments for the selected view (day/week/month).
Open the appointment and click the Print/Receipt option. A formatted receipt is generated that you can print or save as PDF to share with the client.
Open the sale, click Checkout, choose the payment method (Cash, Card, UPI, Gift Card, or Split), add a reference if needed, and confirm. The sale status changes to Completed.
Supported: Cash, Card, UPI, Gift Card, and Split (two methods combined in one transaction). You can also record a payment reference number for traceability.
When editing a sale, enter the amount in the Discount field. You can apply it at the overall sale level or per individual line item (service or product).
In the sale or checkout screen, enter the amount in the Tip Amount field before completing payment. Tips are tracked separately and included in staff commission reports.
Open the completed sale and change its status to Refunded. Note the original payment method — the actual refund must be issued through that same channel (cash/card/UPI) manually.
Go to Sales > Daily Sales. Use the date arrows to navigate or click Today. You'll see all transactions, payment method breakdown, and a cash movement summary.
Go to Sales > Quick Sale. Add the services or products, optionally link a client, and proceed to checkout. Quick sales are tracked in daily sales reports alongside appointment-based sales.
Yes — when creating a sale, there is a Tax Amount field. Enter the applicable tax (e.g. GST). The tax is added to the total and shown separately on the receipt.
At checkout, select Split as the payment method. You can then enter how much is paid via each method (e.g. ₹500 cash + ₹300 UPI). Both are recorded in the transaction.
Go to Sales > Sales List. You can filter by date range, client, or status (Draft, Completed, Refunded). Click any sale to view its full itemised details.
A Draft sale has been created but checkout has not been completed — the client has not been charged. Open the sale and click Checkout to finalise the payment.
On the Daily Sales or Sales List page, click Export. You can download as PDF, Excel, or text. Choose your date range before exporting for a scoped report.
Go to Clients > Add. Enter the client's full name (required), phone, and email. They'll appear in the list and can be selected when booking appointments.
In Clients, select the client(s), then Options > Block Customers and provide a reason. Blocked clients can't be added to bookings. You can unblock anytime from the same menu.
Go to Clients > Options > Merge Clients. Select the primary record to keep and the duplicate. The primary absorbs the duplicate's history. This cannot be undone.
Go to Clients > Options > Import Clients. Upload a CSV or Excel file with columns for name, phone, and email. The system validates and imports all valid records.
Click any client in the Clients list to open their profile. You'll see their full booking history, past sales, tags, and any notes linked to their account.
In the Clients list, go to Options > Export. Your client data (name, phone, email, sales, and creation date) will be downloaded as a CSV or Excel file.
Select the client(s) in the Clients list, then Options > Add Tags. You can create custom tags like "VIP", "Regular", or "Bride" to segment clients for marketing campaigns.
Click the client in the Clients list to open their profile, then click Edit. Update their name, phone, email, or any other detail, and save the changes.
Select the client in the Clients list, go to Options > Delete. Confirm the deletion. Note: this permanently removes the client's record and cannot be undone.
Use the search bar at the top of the Clients page. You can search by name, phone number, or email. Results filter in real time as you type.
Go to Team > Add Staff. Fill in name, email, phone, and role. Then configure which services they provide, their branch location, working hours, and wage/commission settings.
In the staff profile or Team > Shifts, add shifts with start/end times per day. Use Repeating Shifts to automatically set a recurring weekly schedule.
In the staff profile, go to the Commissions section and set the rate per service. Commissions are auto-calculated from completed sales and visible in the Pay Runs section.
Go to Team > Pay Runs. Create a new pay run for the period, review each staff member's hours and commissions, then mark it completed once wages are processed.
In the staff profile or shift calendar, click Add Time Off. Set the date range and reason. The staff member won't be available for bookings during that period.
Open the staff profile and go to the Services section. Enable the services this staff member can perform. Only enabled services will appear when booking them on the calendar.
Go to Team > Timesheets. Select the staff member and date range. You'll see a breakdown of hours worked per day based on their logged shifts and appointments.
In the staff profile, go to the Wages section. You can set an hourly rate or a fixed salary. This is used in Pay Runs to calculate the total wages owed.
Yes — in the staff profile, go to the Locations section. You can assign them to one or multiple branches. They'll only appear on the calendar for bookings at their assigned locations.
Go to Team > Repeating Shifts. Set the days and times for recurring shifts and choose the repeat frequency. This automatically creates shift entries for future weeks.
Go to Marketing > Settings. Enter your Phone Number ID, WABA ID, App ID, and Access Token from Meta Business Manager. Once verified, you can create templates and run campaigns.

Go to Marketing > Templates > Create. Add the name, category (Marketing/Utility/Authentication), language, and body text. Optionally add a header and buttons. Submit for Meta approval — usually takes minutes to 24 hours.
Check the rejection reason in the template details. Common causes: promotional language in a Utility template, policy violation, or unclear variable usage. Revise the content and resubmit.
Go to Marketing > Campaigns > Create. Select an approved template, upload contacts via Excel, set the batch size, and launch. Monitor delivery in real time on the campaign page.
Open the campaign from Marketing > Campaigns. You'll see Sent, Delivered, Read, Failed, and Blocked counts. The Marketing Dashboard shows daily message volume across all campaigns.
Open the campaign from Marketing > Campaigns and click the Pause button. The campaign status will change to Paused and no further messages will be sent until you resume it.
Your daily limit is set by Meta based on your WhatsApp Business account tier. You can view your current daily limit in Marketing > Settings under your WhatsApp Config details.

Quality rating (Green/Yellow/Red) reflects how recipients respond to your messages. Green = good standing. Yellow = some blocks/complaints. Red = high complaints — may restrict sending. Avoid sending too frequently to unresponsive contacts.

When creating a template, select Image, Video, or Document as the header type and upload the file. The media is uploaded to Meta and a handle is generated. This is required before you can submit the template for approval.

Go to Marketing > Webhooks or open a specific campaign's detail page. Each message shows its delivery event: Sent, Delivered, Read, Failed, or Blocked — along with the timestamp for each status update.
Go to Catalog > Services > Add. Enter the name, category, price, and duration. Assign which staff can perform it. The service will appear when booking appointments.
Go to Catalog > Products > Add. Fill in the product name, price, and stock quantity. Products can be sold at checkout alongside services.
Go to Catalog > Memberships > Add. Define the name, price, included services or credits, and validity period. Clients can be enrolled at checkout.
Go to Catalog > Gift Cards and create a card with a fixed value. At checkout, clients pay using "Gift Card" as the payment method. Balances are tracked automatically.
Go to Catalog > Categories > Add. Create categories like Hair, Skin, or Nails. When adding a service, assign it to the relevant category so it's easy to find during booking.
Go to Catalog > Services, find the service, and click Edit. Update the price field and save. The new price will apply to all future bookings and sales.
Go to Catalog > Services, find the service, and click Edit. Update the Duration (in minutes) and save. The new duration will be used when calculating appointment end times.
Go to Catalog > Services, find the service, and click Delete. Confirm the action. Deleting a service won't affect historical sales or past appointments that included it.
Go to Settings > Business Profile. Update your salon name, location, contact info, and logo. For multi-location setups, each branch can have its own profile.
On the login page, click "Forgot Password" and enter your registered email. You'll receive a reset link. Check your spam folder if you don't see it within a few minutes.
Go to Team > Add Staff, fill in their details, and save. They'll receive an invitation to join your account with their assigned role and permissions.
Go to Apps. Add-ons: Payments, Premium Support, Insights, Google Rating Boost, Client Loyalty, Data Connector. Integrations: Google Reserve, Facebook, and Meta. Click any to connect or learn more.
Go to Settings > Locations or Business Profile and add a new branch. Each branch can have its own staff, services, and calendar. Staff can be assigned to one or multiple branches.
Go to Settings > Account or your User Profile. Update your email or phone number. You may need to verify the new contact via OTP before the change is applied.
On the Login page, click "Continue with Google" or the equivalent social login option. On first use, you'll be redirected to authorise access, and your account will be linked for future logins.
Click the invitation link sent by your manager. It will open the Join Business page where you create your staff profile. Once approved, you'll have access based on your assigned role.
Go to Reports > Revenue. Choose a period (7 days, 30 days, or a custom range). You'll see a chart with daily revenue vs target vs previous period, plus a total summary card.
Go to Reports > Services. You'll see a breakdown of each service by number of bookings and revenue earned, helping you identify your highest-performing offerings.
Go to Reports > Appointments. Filter by date range to see total bookings, completed, cancelled, and no-show counts. You can also see trends over time.
Go to Reports > Staff. You'll see each staff member's total appointments, revenue generated, and average per visit. This helps compare performance across your team.
Go to Analytics > Gift Cards. You'll see total gift cards issued, redeemed, outstanding balances, and a list of all gift card transactions in the selected period.
Go to Analytics > Memberships. You'll see active memberships, expired ones, and a list of enrolled clients with their plan details and remaining credits or validity.
On any report page, click the Export button and choose PDF or Excel (XLSX). Set your date range before exporting to get a scoped report for the period you need.
Go to Analytics > Payments. You'll see a breakdown of revenue by payment method (Cash, Card, UPI, Gift Card, Split) for any selected date range.
The calendar highlights conflicts. Either choose a different staff member or adjust the shift timing first. Staff can only be booked during their defined working hours.
Go to Clients, find the client, and choose Options > Unblock Customers. Confirm the unblock. They can then be added to new appointments.
Error 131049 is a Meta recipient-level rate limit — the recipient's number has been messaged too frequently. Wait 24 hours and retry. This cannot be bypassed by the platform.
Open the draft sale, verify the items, and click Checkout to process payment. The status will change to Completed once payment is confirmed.
Check three things: (1) the service is assigned in their profile, (2) they have a shift on that day, and (3) their branch matches the booking location. All three must be set correctly.
Use Clients > Options > Merge Clients. Select the primary (correct) record — it absorbs the duplicate's data. This cannot be undone, so verify before confirming.

Check: (1) invalid phone numbers in your contact list, (2) numbers not registered on WhatsApp, (3) Meta quality rating — if it's Red, sending is restricted. Review webhook events for per-message failure reasons.
Refresh the page first. Check your internet connection. If it persists, wait a minute — there may be a brief backend outage. If the issue continues for more than 5 minutes, contact support.

Wait 30 seconds and click Resend OTP. If it still doesn't arrive, check if the phone number is correct. If using an internet-based SIM, SMS delivery may be delayed — try again or use the Google login option.
Ensure your business timezone is correctly set in Settings > Business Profile. All appointment times are displayed based on the salon's configured timezone, not your device timezone.
Verify the gift card code is correct and has a remaining balance. Check in Catalog > Gift Cards. If the balance is zero or the card is expired, the payment method won't be applicable.

Check that the service is included in the client's membership plan. Also verify the membership is still active and hasn't expired. Go to the client's profile to review their current membership status.
